provenance.source_corpus: Endophytic colonization and Æeld responses of hybrid spruce seedlings after inoculation with plant growth-promoting rhizobacteria

source_spans.text: In addition, spruce growth was significantly enhanced by bacterial inoculation at certain sites.
In addition, spruce growth was significantly enhanced by bacterial inoculation at certain sites.
These three strains increased seedling dry weight up to 57% above noninoculated controls at five of the nine outplanting sites.
These three strains increased seedling dry weight up to 57% above noninoculated controls at five of the nine outplanting sites.
Seedling growth inhibition due to bacterial inoculation was detected at some sites.
Seedling survival was not affected by PGPR inoculation except at site SMS, where survival of inoculated seedlings was 21–36% greater than controls
